dialogueChunks.intro: I can't believe it worked. {player_name}, they're leaving peacefully.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} packed up their camp this morning and headed north. No raid, no blood. {target_npc} watched them go from the watchtower and said one of them turned back and raised a hand: not a threat, just a farewell. The settlement still doesn't know the full story, but nobody died. That's enough for me.
dialogueChunks.outro: You walked into a situation that could have ended in massacre and found a way through. I won't forget that.

playerResponses.text: A raised hand is a start. Maybe someday it'll be an open one.
It's done. Let's hope both sides remember this.
Just make sure the guards don't chase after them.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... the {enemy_type} elder sent something for you.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It's a carved token. {target_npc} says it means 'remembered friend' in their culture. Not enemy, not ally: friend. The settlement is confused, a few people are angry, but most are just relieved the fighting stopped. Some are even starting to ask questions about why the {enemy_type} were here in the first place. That's more than I ever hoped for.
dialogueChunks.outro: You didn't just end a conflict. You planted a seed of understanding. Take this token and what little we can offer: both are earned many times over.

playerResponses.text: I'll carry this token with pride. Thank you.
Understanding takes time. But today was a good step.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's quiet now. A different kind of quiet than before: not the silence of fear, but the stillness after something heavy finally lifts.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} moderates made it back to their people and convinced the war party to stand down. {target_npc} told me this morning that a small delegation came to the edge of {location} and left a bundle of {quest_item} at the border marker. A gift, not a theft. First time that's ever happened in the history of this settlement.
dialogueChunks.outro: I spent my whole life hating them. Now I don't know what I feel. But it's something better than hate. Here, take this: you've earned it.

playerResponses.text: Something better than hate is a fine place to start.
Change is uncomfortable. But it beats the alternative.
